contract.goal: Add a command -v preflight to each ev-loop-* and ev-run skill body. Fails loud + actionable if loom/guild/griot deps aren't on PATH. Belt-and-suspenders alongside the marketplace dependencies cascade — catches the runtime case where a user disabled a dep plugin mid-session.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the 3 ev skill bodies (ev-loop-confidence, ev-loop-interactive, ev-run) gains a `## Preflight` section early. Block shape: single Bash("command -v loom guild griot >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo '<actionable msg>' >&2; exit 1; }") invocation.
Error message names all three deps + the remediation command (claude plugin enable loom@krambuhl guild@krambuhl griot@krambuhl) inline.
V11 (cli/skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts) still passes — the new Bash() calls use bare loom/guild/griot per W4 idiom.
rules_applied: Loud-fail idiom (matches the substrate's other loud-fail patterns: checkin-already-exists, sync-shared drift).
Belt-and-suspenders: marketplace dependencies cascade is install/enable-time correctness; this preflight is runtime correctness.
disqualifiers: No changes to griot-*, guild-*, loom-* skill bodies (those plugins don't fan out to multi-plugin deps).
No new lint/test for the preflight pattern itself (the V11 grep tripwire already covers the bare-command invariant).
No invocation-order changes (preflight first; rest of skill body unchanged).

actions: Added '## Preflight' section to skills/ev-loop-confidence/SKILL.md right after the intro paragraph and before '## Substrate compositions.' Section: brief rationale (marketplace deps handle install/enable; this catches runtime disabled-mid-session) + Bash() invocation in a fenced code block + stop-on-non-zero instruction.
Added matching '## Preflight' section to skills/ev-loop-interactive/SKILL.md at the same structural position.
Added '## Preflight' section to skills/ev-run/SKILL.md right after the intro paragraph and before '## Process.' Same shape, with skill-specific 'ev-run requires...' message.

Each Bash() invocation: `command -v loom guild griot >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo '<skill> requires loom + guild + griot plugins on PATH. Enable them with: claude plugin enable loom@krambuhl guild@krambuhl griot@krambuhl' >&2; exit 1; }`. Bare-command refs throughout — V11-compatible.
npm test (full suite) → 581/581 passed (no count change — W5 adds doc content, no new test assertions).
npx vitest run cli/skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts → 27/27 passed (V11 still green; verifies the new Bash() calls don't reintroduce bin/<cli> patterns).

Invoked /guild-validate with contract-fit (the only evaluator the panel-spec maps for *.md changes). Approved with one minor non-blocking nit: the prose 'Before doing anything else' sits after the intro paragraph rather than as the literal first section. Contract permits this ('inserted between intro and Process') — within tolerance.

changes_since_previous: W4 (af360e7) rewrote all skill bodies to invoke CLIs as bare commands + landed V11 tripwire. W5 adds the belt-and-suspenders runtime preflight on top — the marketplace dependencies cascade is install/enable-time correctness; the preflight is the runtime check that catches the user-disabled-a-dep-mid-session edge case.
notes_for_pr: Preflight semantics: marketplace dependencies cascade ensures the deps are installed + enabled at plugin-install time. The preflight in each ev-* skill body covers the runtime case where a user runs `claude plugin disable loom@krambuhl` after installing ev — without this check, the skill would crash mid-flight on the first `Bash("loom ...")` call with a generic 'command not found' error. The preflight makes the failure self-explanatory with a copy-paste remediation.

Error message includes the skill name (e.g. 'ev-loop-interactive requires...') so the operator knows which skill blew up without having to trace the stack. All three messages name all three deps explicitly + the full `claude plugin enable` command.

Bash() invocation uses bare commands (loom/guild/griot) per W4 — V11 grep tripwire still passes. If a future maintainer accidentally reverted a Bash() in the preflight to bin/<cli>, V11 would catch it.

Section placement: after the intro paragraph and before the first substantive section (## Substrate compositions / ## Process). Matches the natural reading order — readers see the preflight expectation before diving into the loop body.
